content.full_text:
FPPC

SEP 17 2:31 PM '90

360 S. Euclid Ave., #115
Pasadena, Ca. 91101
Sept. 12, 1990


Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St., Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Subj: Emergency Request for Expedited Advice: L.A. County
        First Supervisorial District Race

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     Los Angeles Councilwoman Gloria Molina has filed a Statement of
Intention to be a candidate for the 1st Supervisorial District in
Los Angeles County.  Subsequent to that filing, the U.S. Court of
Appeals has indefinitely postponed that election.

     Councilwoman Molina still intends to run for the Supervisorial
office, whenever the election is finally scheduled.  However, given
the uncertainty of the election date, some question has arisen as to
the continuing validity of the Councilwoman's existing 501 filing.

     Our reading of Government Code Section 85200, which requires
that "an individual who intends to be a candidate for an elective
office shall file with the commission a statement signed under
penalty of perjury of intention to be a candidate for a specific
office", is that a date certain for the election to the specific
office is assumed, but is not explicitly required, by the Code.
Although the Appeals Court action renders the date of the election
uncertain, there can be no doubt that there will, eventually, be an
election for the 1st District Supervisorial position.

     Therefore, unless we hear otherwise from your office by Sept.
21, we plan, on that date, to organize and register a committee
(with myself as Treasurer) supporting Councilwoman Molina's election
to the Board of Supervisors, that election to be on a date set by
the courts.  Further, we plan to begin soliciting funds for that
committee at that time.

     Given the urgency of this issue, we hope and respectfully
request an early response.  You can call me at home (818 449-7655)
or at work (213 414-6915).

                                        Sincerely yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jonathan S. Fuhrman
                                        Treasurer, Molina
                                        City Council Reelection '93

[The image appears to be a very faint or low-contrast document that is largely illegible. While there appears to be text content organized in paragraphs and possibly tables throughout the document, the text is too faint or degraded for me to reliably transcribe the specific words and content with confidence. To provide an accurate transcription following the rules given, I would need a clearer, higher-contrast version of this document.]
